title: equisetum variegatum subsp. alaskanum
intro: Equisetum variegatum subsp. alaskanum, also known as Alaskan horsetail, is a subspecies of the Equisetum variegatum plant, which is a perennial plant belonging to the horsetail family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of silica, which is beneficial for skin and hair health. The health benefits of Alaskan horsetail include its ability to improve skin and hair conditions, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. Alaskan horsetail can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and has been used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and address various health issues.
